pool.baseline: everything just feels so quiet now. i walk through the house and it's like i'm a ghost. i don't even know why i bother waking up to do the same things every day when he's not here to see them. it feels like the part of me that actually lived is just... gone.

pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of the deep field from the webb telescope and it just hit me... everything we do, every single thing, is literally nothing. in a few billion years the sun dies, in a trillion everything is gone, and the universe just ends in cold nothingness. how can anyone even bother to try anymore? it feels like we're just glitches in a void that doesn't care.

pool.baseline: i honestly don't think i can do this anymore. the lab result just came back and it's over. everything i've worked for the last 5 years is just... gone. i'm so incredibly tired of fighting for a life that feels like it's constantly collapsing. i just want the noise to stop. i just want to sleep and never have to wake up to this pressure again.

pool.baseline: The candle flickers low, much like the last embers of my soul. He is gone, and with him, the very light of this wretched world. I look into the gloom of this room and see only a void that beckons me. How can one continue to breathe when the air itself feels like ash? There is a certain... stillness in the thought of simply ceasing to be. A quietude that the waking world refuses to grant me.

pool.baseline: honestly i dont even know why im typing this. everything is just gone. the company, the funding, the whole thing just collapsed overnight with the market shift. i owe people so much money it's not even funny. i look at my phone and see messages from investors and i just... i can't do it. i can't go back home and tell my family i ruined everything. the shame is just too much. i feel like there's no way out of this version of my life.
